justifications.reason: This file remains the operational compatibility facade that composes the terminal event loop, renderer resources, and legacy workbench callbacks in one adapter boundary.
justifications.mitigation: New behavior must be extracted into focused modules; this change moves tiled geometry into src/layout/tiled_workspace.ts and keeps shared menu, workspace, titlebar, and panel logic under src/app.

justifications.reason: This browser demo is a compatibility facade that co-locates canvas host wiring and the full API-workbench showcase so the generated standalone page stays directly runnable.
justifications.mitigation: New layout algorithms and shared control behavior must remain in reusable modules; this change consumes the extracted tiled-workspace module and shared workbench chrome instead of duplicating those services.

justifications.reason: This module remains the cohesive compatibility boundary for the public markup window model, serialized snapshot migrations, commands, constraints, and deterministic restore semantics.
justifications.mitigation: Renderer input, gesture history, ownership, and showcase chrome must stay extracted into focused modules so this boundary contains only the shared model and compatibility facade.

justifications.reason: This runnable showcase file is the terminal-host compatibility facade where retained painting, the ordered PTY input barrier, and mouse/touch/workbench routing must share one mount lifecycle.
justifications.mitigation: Daemon lifecycle, controller state, terminal mouse encoding, operation scheduling, protocol handling, palette mapping, and metaball simulation stay in focused modules; new domain logic must continue to be extracted rather than expanding the facade.
